content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

November 15, 2001

Lorie A. Campos
Burke, Williams & Sorenson, LLP
611 West Sixth Street, Suite 2500
Los Angeles, CA 90017-3102

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-01-264

Dear Ms. Campos:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding section 84303 of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

Generally, the Commission provides written advice to public officials so long as
the requestor submits all relevant information regarding the advice request. (§ 83114.)
However, prudence suggests we not provide written advice in certain circumstances.
(regulation 18329(b)(8).) Among the factors considered by the Commission in
determining whether or not to issue written advice are: (1) whether or not the conduct in
question has already occurred, and (2) whether providing written advice is in the public
interest.

According to telephone information we obtained from you on November 9, 2001,
the conduct about which you requested advice has already occurred. Further, you note
that the precise question about which you request advice is the subject of current
litigation. The Commission believes that it is not in the public interest for this Agency to
become involved in matters which are subject

_______________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. W-01-264
Page No. 2

to pending litigation. Based upon these factors, the Commission must decline to issue
written advice in this matter.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
